Real Testing Notes: Best for: Designers wanting full control. Best design freedom but steepest learning curve.
column12: Steepest learning curve, can get expensive, e-commerce features basic compared to Shopify, can get slow with complex sites, requires design knowledge for best results.
column13: TESTED: Built portfolio site with CMS blog. Design freedom was incredible (could implement any design). CMS handled blog posts beautifully. However, learning curve was brutal (40+ hours to feel proficient). E-commerce features were basic compared to Shopify. Hosting was fast but got pricey with CMS plan ($23/month). Perfect if you're a designer or want complete control, use Squarespace if you want easier.

column12: modern landing pages. Best for quick launches with modern design.
column13: Limited CMS features compared to Webflow, e-commerce basic, fewer integrations, newer platform (fewer resources/tutorials), can get slow with complex pages.
column14: TESTED: Built product landing page in 4 hours (vs 20 hours in Webflow). Component system was intuitive, modern templates were gorgeous. However, CMS features were limited compared to Webflow (couldn't do complex content modeling). E-commerce was basic (Stripe only, limited product options). Perfect for quick landing pages and portfolios, Webflow is better for complex content sites.

column12: Best for: Beginners wanting beautiful sites quickly. Easiest to use but least design freedom.
column13: Limited design flexibility (stuck with templates), can look template-y"
column17: TESTED: Built restaurant website in 3 hours. Templates were beautiful and easy to customize. Blog features were solid (scheduling, categories, SEO). However, felt constrained by templates (couldn't implement custom designs). E-commerce had transaction fees (0-3%) on top of Stripe fees. Perfect for beginners who want beautiful sites fast, use Webflow if you want design control.

Limitations: Best for: Simple one-page landing pages. Cheapest option for basic landing pages.
Real Testing Notes: Very limited features (no CMS, no e-commerce), design flexibility minimal, can't export data, not suitable for complex sites, free tier has branding.
column12: TESTED: Built simple lead capture landing page in 30 minutes. Could not be easier for one-pagers. However, trying to add a blog was impossible (no CMS). E-commerce required embedding third-party tools (clunky). Perfect for simple landing pages (link in bio, portfolio, event page), but use Webflow/Squarespace for anything more complex.

Real Testing Notes: Best for: Lead generation landing pages. Optimized for conversions but expensive for what you get.
column12: Very expensive for features, limited to landing pages, no full website capabilities, can feel restrictive, template-based (limited design freedom).
column13: TESTED: Built webinar registration landing page. Templates were conversion-optimized (high-converting designs). Lead capture and integrations worked flawlessly. However, at $49/month for just landing pages, felt expensive compared to Carrd ($19/year) or Framer ($15/month). Perfect if you need high-converting landing pages and budget isn't a concern, but there are cheaper options.
